title: OP - sign cover email
excerpt: Hi there - I have set up a an integration with DocuGen and it is working great but I need to edit some things on the cover email.  It is pulling through the item name for the relevant data, which I don’t want to include …

posts.author: JulesD (Julie Derevycka)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there - I have set up a an integration with DocuGen and it is working great but I need to edit some things on the cover email. It is pulling through the item name for the relevant data, which I don’t want to include and also the account name needs to change from a user name to a company name. So that the bottom of the email reads: if you have any questions contact COMPANY NAME. Can you please help. Many thanks, J

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Julie,

Because Docugen is a 3rd party app, I’d recommend reaching out to the app’s developers directly. You can reach them via support@docugen.io - hope this helps in some way!

posts.author: JulesD (Julie Derevycka)
posts.cookedHtml: I think the cover letter is created by OPSign and not DocuGen - do you have a support contact for them?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Julie,

Is this the app you’re using in the platform?

If so, you can find their support email here → contact@op.today

posts.author: samicaracand (Sami Caracand (DocuGen))
posts.cookedHtml:
@JulesD I have good news! We’re releasing e-signatures as a new feature in DocuGen starting next week. With DocuGen Sign you can:

  • Collect verified e-signatures protected by a digital seal (like DocuSign)
  • Send documents to multiple signers
  • Automate document sending using automations

We’re granting access to DocuGen Sign early adopters as soon as next week (10 June.) If you would like access, please fill out this form:

forms.monday.com

DocuGen Sign access request

Please fill out this form to request early access to DocuGen Sign. We will process requests in the order we receive them.
